data.title: Nigel Coe Reiterates Hold on MSC Industrial Amid Solid Q3 Results but Limited Upside Catalysts
data.description:
Wolfe Research analyst Nigel Coe has maintained their neutral stance on MSM stock, giving a Hold rating yesterday.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Nigel Coe has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to MSC Industrial’s solid but not exceptional performance. The company delivered healthy third-quarter sales growth, supported largely by pricing power and modest volume gains, while also expanding gross margin through favorable price-cost dynamics.

At the same time, operating margin improvement, though positive, remains incremental and partly influenced by one-time items such as litigation-related costs and recent headcount actions. Taken together, the results indicate a stable, well-managed business, but without a sufficiently compelling catalyst or valuation upside to justify a more aggressive rating than Hold.

Coe covers the Industrials sector, focusing on stocks such as Johnson Controls, Madison Air Solutions Corp. Class A, and Otis Worldwide. According to TipRanks, Coe has an average return of 8.2% and a 54.29% success rate on recommended stocks.

data.title: Tesla: Strong Q2 Deliveries Support Core Franchise but Valuation Keeps Risk/Reward Neutral, Warrants Hold
data.description:
William Blair analyst Jed Dorsheimer has maintained their neutral stance on TSLA stock, giving a Hold rating on June 26.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

High conviction TSLA bears now have this Tradr ETF

Jed Dorsheimer has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, starting with Tesla’s strong second-quarter deliveries meaningfully exceeding market expectations, driven largely by robust Model 3 and Model Y demand across key global regions. While this reinforces the durability of Tesla’s core auto franchise, it also sets a higher performance bar going forward, making it harder for future quarters to deliver comparable positive surprises.

At the same time, Jed notes that some legacy models, such as the Model S and Model X, are nearing the end of their demand cycle, suggesting that recent strength in these vehicles may not be sustainable. Balancing the solid near-term operational execution against potential normalization in growth and the already demanding valuation, he concludes that the risk/reward profile is currently neutral, warranting a Hold rather than a more aggressive rating.

In another report released on June 26, Morgan Stanley also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $415.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Initiates Buy Rating on Caris, Highlighting Caris Detect Launch and Early Positive Economics
data.description:
Caris Life Sciences, Inc., the Healthcare sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst yesterday. Analyst Evie Koslosky from Goldman Sachs maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a $27.00 price target.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Evie Koslosky has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to the launch of Caris Detect, a multi-cancer early detection test that leverages whole genome and transcriptome sequencing alongside advanced AI to identify early-stage disease. He views this product as a gateway for Caris to access a substantial new market in multi-cancer early detection, significantly expanding the company’s commercial opportunity.

Koslosky also notes that the test is supported by Achieve 1 study data showing strong specificity and meaningful sensitivity in stage I/II cancers, which underpins clinical credibility as adoption with partners such as Everlywell begins. In addition, the planned $3,500 list price is expected to yield at least neutral gross margins initially, with room for margin improvement as volumes scale, reinforcing his positive outlook on the company’s earnings potential and justifying the Buy recommendation.

In another report released today, BTIG also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $32.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Guardant Health: Buy Rating Reaffirmed as UnitedHealth Coverage Expands Shield Access; 12-Month Price Target Maintained at $165
data.description:
Guardant Health, the Healthcare sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst yesterday. Analyst Evie Koslosky from Goldman Sachs maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a $165.00 price target.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Evie Koslosky has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Guardant Health’s strengthening commercial outlook for its Shield colorectal cancer screening test. The recent UnitedHealth decision to cover Shield meaningfully broadens access beyond Medicare, opening up reimbursement for millions of commercially insured patients aged 45 to 64 and supporting both higher realized pricing and test volumes.

Koslosky’s view is that this expanded coverage, effective August 2026 and reaching about 40 million additional Americans, should reinforce Guardant’s leadership in blood-based cancer testing despite emerging competition. Combined with direct-to-consumer initiatives, strategic partnerships, and supportive clinical guidelines, these tailwinds underpin the unchanged 12‑month price target of $165 and justify maintaining a Buy recommendation on the stock.

In another report released today, Bernstein also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $200.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Kroger Buy Rating Reaffirmed as Giant Eagle Acquisition Strengthens Growth Outlook; Price Target Maintained at $72
data.description:
Goldman Sachs analyst Leah Jordan maintained a Buy rating on Kroger Company yesterday and set a price target of $72.00.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Leah Jordan has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that highlight Kroger’s strengthened strategic position and financial flexibility. The planned acquisition of Giant Eagle expands Kroger’s presence into several adjacent states, adding meaningful scale while requiring only limited divestitures to satisfy regulators, which supports long-term growth prospects.

The deal structure, funded largely with cash and modest assumed liabilities, aligns with Kroger’s already low leverage and sizeable cash reserves, allowing the company to maintain its capital allocation priorities and balance sheet discipline. Management expects the transaction to be earnings accretive in the second full year after closing, supported by synergy capture and the deployment of Kroger’s established e-commerce and data capabilities across the enlarged footprint, and Leah Jordan keeps the price target unchanged at $72, reflecting confidence in this value creation path.
